sourceMovie.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pacific Rim
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Transformers: Age of Extinction clicked for you, Pacific Rim (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Transformers: Age of Extinction?
faq.answer: Try Pacific Rim, Transformers, Alita: Battle Angel—each shares DNA with Transformers: Age of Extinction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Transformers: Age of Extinction?
faq.answer: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Transformers: Age of Extinction part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Transformers Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
